entry.title: Re: dynamic selection disabled in VA05
entry.content: The dynamic selection in VA05 is controlled by program logic as I know, not just user parameters; it's disabled for certain output types or variants, so enabling it may require a custom variant or SAP note implementation.

entry.title: Re: Issue in enabling the Horizontal scrollbar in the Sap...
entry.content:
@junwuThanks for the reply. In my case i don't see the horizontal scroll bar and columns are getting wrapped.

I tried using tabletype="Table" but still same issue

entry.title: Pull messages from Solace Queue using the AEM adapter in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Pull messages from Solace Queue using the AEM adapter in CPI

 

  1. Configure the AEM adapter with the details from service created in cluster manager of Solace

Provide the config details from above snapshot & maintain them on AEM channel config

Create the password as secure parameter in Security Material

We are trying to consume messages from the queue created

The testing is successful. After deploying the artifact, the messages are consumed from the queue

Regards,
Rohini Potham

entry.title: Re: SUM prerequisite Extended check is stuck at phase CON...
entry.content: It’s likely due to unresolved addon/component conflicts or corrupt stack XML; regenerate the stack file in Maintenance Planner, recheck addon compatibility, and ensure latest SUM version is used.

entry.title: How to Upload Project Budget in Company Code Currency?
entry.content:
How to upload Project Budget in the company code currency?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Upload Project Budget in Company Code Currency?
entry.content:
To Upload project budget in company code currency, make use of the below respective fields : 

Amounts and Currencies  HSL  AmountInCompanyCodeCurrency

Refer to the below SAP HELP documentation for the same

Fields for Financial Plan Data Import

How to Import Financial Plan data 

Currencies

entry.title: How can I find the source field of a target field in S4Hana cloud?
entry.content:
In the billing document Header Detail, under Taxes, I have a field called "VAT Registration No.". I need to know what the source field of this field is and which business partner role it comes from (Payer, BillTo, ShipTo). 

The general question of how to find the source field of a target field frequently comes up in other situations, too.

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA Flowgraphs: What SQL Statements are executed?
entry.content: To see the SQL statements executed by a HANA Flowgraph, you can enable SQL trace (ST05 or PlanViz) or use Performance Analysis in the Web IDE; this lets you capture and analyze the generated SQL with minimal effort.

entry.title: Steps to push messages from Postman to Solace - SAP CPI scenarios
entry.content:
Create a demo trial account for solace pub sub

Navigate to Cluster Manager & create a service

Open the service & click on connect tab as shown below

The below library shows the host & connection details to connect to the service

 

Create a queue & topic on event broker

Create a topic subscription as below

Go to Postman & use the URL, credentials to hit to the topic end point “rohini”

 

Navigate to the queue & check the messages have successfully pushed from postman to solace queue

 

Regards,

Rohini Potham

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:

Be sure to join the many Interest Groups | SAP Community

Best of luck!

entry.title: Re: How to change the tax classification for professional service orders using BAdI “Sales Item Tax
entry.content:
Hi Team,
Good day!
Thank you for the detailed step by step guide. Its really helpful when comes to adding custom fields.

Adding to this , I have a query on custom field. Lets say,

  1. We need 1 Extra column in Billing Plan level. So that we can post some  Text over there. the Same Can be printed in Invoice Print form.
  2. for example if i have 10 billing plans in 1 SO then i can enter Description for 10 Billing Plans. 
  3. If i Release 2 Billing Plans for one Invoice those description should flow to Billing Document. 
  4. The Description of Billing Plan we can use in Printout level.

Thank you for the help in advance.
Regards,
Ashish Kumar Patra

entry.title: Re: Business Volume calculation in Condition Contract Man...
entry.content:
In WB2R_BUSVOL, select Output Level Business Volume as Single Record and check the Invoice documents used to arrive the values then you may get the details. 

If multiple invoice documents are posted for the contract validity period then the values will be posted against the contract number

entry.title: Re: Brazil - Alphanumerical CNPJ
entry.content: yes, saw this one, but nothing about S4HANA yet

entry.title: Re: How can I find the source field of a target field in S4Hana cloud?
entry.content: To find the source of the "VAT Registration No." in the billing document header (Taxes) in SAP, first use F1 help on the field to identify the underlying table and field name. Then, check the Business Partner master data (transaction BP) for the Payer and Bill-to Party roles, specifically under the "Tax Numbers" tab, as the VAT number likely originates from one of these. If not found, explore the tax configuration in SPRO. The field is usually linked to the VAT registration number maintained in the relevant business partner's master data.

entry.title: Issue on Label Printing: the character 'O' (letter o) looks like a '0' (zero)
entry.content: We have a problem with a certain label used for storage units. Whenever it is printed, the character 'O' (letter o) looks like a '0' (zero). It seems like a font issue used with the label template. The label is in IPL language printing on Intermec printers. Would you be able to provide troubleshooting steps for both label template generated in SAP Script and Smartforms?

entry.title: Re: Issue in Storage Location Determination in SAP ECC PP...
entry.content:
Hi @dineshudl 

Do you work with (e)WM? Your components are assigned to an operation of the routing. The operation has a work center. The work center has a supply area. The supply area is linked to a storage location.

If you have this setup, the supply area storage location can be determined and used as well.

The determination happens in a logical predefined order of course. More information on it via SAP note 137225. If your behaviour is different, please check user exits, badi's or check with SAP.

https://me.sap.com/notes/137225/E

Hope this helps.

Kind regards,
Jens Koyen

entry.title: Re: Issue in Storage Location Determination in SAP ECC PP...
entry.content: Hi Jens, I don't use any of the setup you mentioned above. If I delete the first Production Version, system determine storage location from second PV. Only if I have multiple PV's, it always goes for first PV Storage Location but other parameter like BOM and routing operation are determined from second PV. Regards, Dinesh Kumar V.

Pavan Kumar M

entry.title: Multiplication of colunm Sap Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello all!!

I'm new and need some heIp. Is there a way to multiply the values of a measure in a data action? For example:

Equip  FDS  TYPE

A        0,97  PARALEL

B        0,66 PARALEL

C      0,88  SERIE

TOTAL = 0,97 * 0,66 * 0,88

I've tried this way but it didn't work

VARIABLEMEMBER #total OF [d/Measures]

DATA([d/Measures] = #total) = 1

 

FOREACH [d/TYPE]

DATA([d/Measures] = #total) = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #total  *  RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] ="FDS")

ENDFOR

entry.title: Fixed asset valuation - 0L and 2L ledgers both need to be filled in?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm currently working in the "Manage Fixed Assets" app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and I’m a bit confused about the Valuation section of the asset master data. I noticed that there are multiple valuation areas assigned to an asset, linked to different ledgers (for example, 0L and 2L). Here's a screenshot for reference:

My questions are:

Do we need to fill in both ledgers? What happens if we don't?

What is the difference between these ledgers in this context?

Is there a best practice or recommendation for which valuation areas should be maintained, and which ones can be left alone if unused?

Why does Ledger 2L appear multiple times with different depreciation areas (15, 16, 32)?

Any help to better understand how to work with this screen and when/why to fill in each line would be greatly appreciated!

https://api.sap.com/integrationflow/Replicate_SAP_SuccessFactors_Users_to_ServiceNow_User

Hope it helps.

Best regards,

Sirui

entry.title: #COE#SW01 Copy data from Business Partner custom field to Company Code level
entry.content:
We have a custom field on the business partner containing the customer dunning email address pushed through an api from an external application. Later we will add company codes to the business partner.

How can we automatically sync the data from the custom field on the business partner level to the clerks internet address field on company code level without repushing the data through the interface each time a new company code is added?

entry.title: Re: Timesheet Approval BADI SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud Edition
entry.content: @Łukasz_Sałek , I have already reviewed the provided documentation, but I couldn’t find any information related to the Project Manager approval logic. The only sample code provided seems to be for line managers and junior consultants.

entry.title: Re: Please provide details how to create Purchase info re...
entry.content:
Automating Purchase Orders: Step-by-Step Configuration Guide

Automating purchase orders (PO) is a game-changer for streamlining procurement processes. By enabling seamless integration between systems and minimizing manual intervention, businesses can save time, reduce errors, and enhance supplier relationships. This guide covers the essential steps, including configuring ALE data, activating automatic PO in item categories, and maintaining material sources and purchase info records.

With SAP’s powerful tools like VOV7, ME01, and ME11, automating POs ensures efficiency and accuracy, making your procurement processes faster, smarter, and more reliable.

Adding ALE Data for Purchase Order in Sales Organization

Application Link Enabling (ALE) is essential for integrating and transferring data between systems in distributed environments. Configuring ALE for purchase orders ensures seamless communication and automation across the sales organization.

ALE (Application Link Enabling) data for purchase orders. Key fields include the sales organization “TATA,” associated plant, purchasing group (001), supplier (900002), storage location (101A), and movement type (101). The setup enables seamless integration for automated POs by linking the sales organization with the purchasing organization and supplier. Additionally, it includes text formatting fields, statistical currency (INR), and options for rebate processing and standard purchase order type (NB).

Step 1: Activate Automatic PO in Item Category (VOV7)

To enable automation, the Automatic PO setting must be activated in the Item Category. This ensures the system generates POs without manual intervention for specific categories.

  • Steps to configure in VOV7:
    1. Navigate to Transaction Code VOV7.
    2. Locate the relevant item category.
    3. Enable the Automatic PO checkbox.
    4. Save the settings.

Step 2: Enable Automatic PO in Business Partner (BP)

The second step involves configuring the Purchasing View in the Business Partner (BP) master data. This step ensures that vendors or suppliers can process automated purchase orders.

  • Steps:
    1. Open the Business Partner module.
    2. Navigate the Purchasing View tab.
    3. Check the Automatic PO option.
    4. Save the changes.

Step 3: Maintain Material Source (ME01)

Defining the material source is critical to identify the supplier for specific materials. Use Transaction Code ME01 for this step.

  • Steps:
    1. Enter the material and plant information in ME01.
    2. Assign a valid supplier source.
    3. Save the source data.

Step 4: Maintain Purchase Info Record (ME11)

Purchase Info Records provides the system with vendor-specific data, including prices, terms, and conditions.

  • Steps:
    1. Access Transaction Code ME11.
    2. Enter details like material, vendor, and purchasing organization.

  1. Specify pricing and validity periods.
  2. Save the record.

Table Summary of Configurations

Step

Transaction Code

Key Action

Activate Automatic PO

VOV7

Enable Automatic PO in Item Category.

Configure Purchasing

BP

Check Automatic PO in Purchasing View.

Define Material Source

ME01

Assign supplier for materials.

Maintain Info Record

ME11

Record vendor details and pricing.

Benefits of Automating Purchase Orders

  1. Time Savings: Reduce manual work in repetitive tasks.
  2. Improved Accuracy: Minimize errors caused by manual entry.
  3. Streamlined Procurement: Ensure timely and efficient purchasing processes.
  4. Enhanced Supplier Relationships: Fulfill orders promptly with fewer discrepancies.

entry.title: How to lock object with odata?
entry.content:
Hi, experts.
How can i lock an object with odata.

as an example : user has a list of deliveries in android app, when it clicked to specific delivery he revieves a list of materials in this delivery.
If a user clicked - i don't want to allow changing this delivery in sap using sap t-code's ( vl*).
I tried using 'ENQUEUE_EVVBLKE', but lock dissapears when the user get the result of responce.
I want to save a lock untill the user comeback to previous screen or save changes.
I've read about etag, but as i understood it's more about lock's before saving to db.

In the BP master, I can find the VAT field, but in this case, my Payer and ShipTo are the same, so I'll need to find an example where they are different. In SPRO, I checked 103110, but no values are assigned to the companies, so I guess it isn't here where it's defined. Not sure where it is.

entry.title: Re: Please provide details how to create Purchase info re...
entry.content: Thank You Naim .

entry.title: Re: When will prices for SQL Anywhere on the SAP Online Store be fixed?
entry.content: Are you looking for the client only?

entry.title: Re: Multiplication of colunm Sap Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@priscilla_barbosa82 

Is there a way to multiply the values of a measure in a data action? -- Yes

What is not clear from your example is where you want to store the result of this multiplication. If you want to store on a equipment member called Total then you have to create the member first. What you have done is use variable members which are not stored and lost after script execution.

So either you can store the result on a new equipment called Total or on a new measure or a new Type. If not then you need to use # for dimensions at least.

Assuming you chose to store it on Measure

MEMBERSET [d/Measures] ="FDS"

MEMBERSET [d/EQIP] =("A","B","C")

DATA([d/Measures] = "Total",[d/EQUIP]="#",[d/Type]="#") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/EQIP] ="A",[d/Type]="PARALEL") * RESULTLOOKUP([d/EQIP] ="B",[d/Type]="PARALEL") * RESULTLOOKUP([d/EQIP] ="C",[d/Type]="SERIE")

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: Decimal place in a calculated field in SAC
entry.content: Hi Nikhil, I found yhe solution , if you create a formula , save and want to change after then you cannot, formatting must be done in the same time

DATA([d/Measures] = "FDMS") = RESULTLOOKUP([d/Measures] = #TOTAL)

entry.title: Re: 1H 2025 Product Release - Deprecation of Canvas Repor...
entry.content:
Hello @lukaszkubiak -

   If you click the link to the blog that is included in that What's New Viewer entry, you'll see the current information.


At present, we've committed to an update in the second half of 2025 to customers around the transition plan and deletion of legacy report tools (Canvas, Table, and now Tiles & Dashboards).  This will then be updated to all customers via direct email notice, this blog will be updated, and the What's New Viewer entry will also be updated.  I hope this addresses your question.

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/retirement-of-canvas-table-tiles-dashboards-and-classic-report-types/ba-p/13619483 

Thanks!
Ben

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cockpit integration suite with internal Server Error
entry.content: hello,

entry.title: Re: What is the use P type variable in ABAP?
entry.content:
Example: 

DATA result TYPE p LENGTH 14 DECIMALS 14.

In ABAP, a packed number (TYPE p) declared with LENGTH 14 means it occupies 14 bytes. The total number of decimal digits that can be stored is calculated as (2*Length – 1), which here gives 27 digits. With DECIMALS set to 14, the number has 27 – 14 = 13 digits before the decimal point. Therefore, the highest number possible is 9,999,999,999,999.99999999999999.

More on the topic here (ABAP Help).

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Cockpit integration suite with internal Server Error
entry.content:
hello, did anyone find the solution? or is it on SAP side problem?
I tried on chrome and edge, clearing cache didnt help

entry.title: How to add one Business Catalog into two Business Roles?
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 

Please help us to know how we can authorize one catalog in two roles.

 

Regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to add one Business Catalog into two Business Roles?
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

 

Hope you're doing pretty well! 

In this case, you have two options: 

  1. Manually open each Business Role and include the Business Catalog. You can check the documentation SAP Help Portal | How to edit active Business Roles.

    OR 

  2. Use the mass update functionality. Check the documentation SAP Help Portal | How to Make Mass Changes to Business Roles.

 

Also, we have the "Leading Business Role" and "Derived Business Role" concept, in case you're not familiar.

It is useful if you need to create several business roles with common characteristics. You can define one business leading role and then derive other business roles from it. This leading role contains the basic settings such as access restrictions, the assigned business catalogs and common restrictions, such as General Accountant or General Planner. The values defined in the leading business role can’t be changed in the derived business role. You can, however, define additional values for the derived role. 

Check SAP Help Portal | How to Create Leading and Derived Business Roles for further information.

 

Kind regards,

SAP Support.

entry.title: Re: Use CRUD Opreations with SAP Datasphere Command-Line-Interface
entry.content:
Hey @Yash02 , unfortunately I do not have a sample JSON. The official SAP documentation provides information on creating replication Flows via CLI (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_DATASPHERE/d0ecd6f297ac40249072a44df0549c1a/6f5c65f209004751aa48f9682ee2ec45.html).

BR
Yves

entry.title: BRF transports
entry.content:
Hi all,

I would like to read the BRF changes contained in a transport.

Sometimes transports are old and would like to check the contents before moving to production.

Here is the scenario:

1- I have a released transport TR1 with a BRF decision table DT1 changes.

2- I would like to export to Excel the BRF changes in DT1, contained in the transport,  before moving to production system

I tried workbench analysis, but it does not give the content. It only shows the changed objects.

I checked all FDT transport classes but was not able to get what i want.

Thanks a lot

entry.title: Re: Fiori Elements - different Value Help for each page w...
entry.content: Hi, I tried this as well (but for object page and filter), but now the field is visible twice in the table settings. For the field on the object page I can use @Consumption.filter.hidden: true, such that it is not shown in 'Adapt filters', but for the list report I could not find anything similar. @ui.lineItem: [{exclude: true}] does not work and if I use @ui.hidden: true, the filter itself also disappears. How did you solve this?

entry.title: Learning metric
entry.content:
Estimado equipo,

Nos estamos comunicando con usted para comprender mejor cómo funciona la métrica de uso de aprendizaje. Nuestro cliente observa un uso excesivo de licencias, y los registros parecen estar vinculados a cursos realizados hace varios años.

Nos gustaría validar cómo se puede reducir el uso del aprendizaje, considerando que muchos usuarios ya no requieren acceso a los cursos comunitarios de SuccessFactors.

gracias de antemano

Atentamente

entry.title: Is it possible to create Table/Canvas Report to link Job Profile to Position?
entry.content:
Hello,

Did anyone manage to create a report for Job Profile that has the same data as in Manage Job Profiles drill down?

Job profile-Job Family-Job Role-Position:

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central SAP SuccessFactors Platform

entry.title: Re: API Provider - Authentication type - Only two options
entry.content:
Even Cloud Integration doesn't support any JWT authentication out of the box for IFlows. Don't ask me why 🙂

And API Management is just passing through the calls to the backend and you can apply policies to interfere with the call. What you are looking at is only the authentication to the catalog service, not to the API provider itself. 

I believe you have to create policies to create a JWT token on API Management and enrich the call with it (see https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/json-web-tokens). Also for the Oauth authentication of the clients there are corresponding policies. 

By the way, this is the discussion section. It seems that the Q&A section has more engagement and is more suitable for questions, maybe you want to post the question there too: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions

entry.title: Re: From User Exits to RAP: Adopting a Clean Core Approach in S/4HANA
entry.content:
@RavdeepSingh : It's very interesting. Thanks for sharing. 

I'm trying to recreate this, but my event consumption class isn't triggered, so my own coding in the method with FOR ENTITY EVENT created FOR SalesOrder~created, when I create a sales order with EML (R_SalesOrderTP, salesorder). The sales order creation with EML is succesfull

Do I have to register something, the global class with the local class or else? I don't get it, how the framework understands, when it has to trigger the coding in the y-class.

Maybe you could describe this in more detail of your blog, please. I realy would like to know.

entry.title: New font for Riyal symbol to be displayed in smartform
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a requirement to display newly launched Riyal symbol in the print. I have downloaded .ttf file from 'https://github.com/emran-alhaddad/Saudi-Riyal-Font?tab=readme-ov-file'.

Then I uploaded the file using RSTXSF01 under the font name 'ZRIYAL' and used the font to create a character format in smartstyle. Then I used character format in smart form but the Riyal symbol is not coming. Whereas when I uploaded the same font in Windows and displayed the value 'E900' using  Alt + 'X' the Riyal symbol is displayed.

In smart form, the value is showing as 'E900' only. I am using device type ARSWIN (to display Arabic text).

Please suggest how to achieve the Riyal symbol.

entry.title: Custom Flexible workflow agent determination via "Manage Teams and Responsibilities"
entry.content:
I've created a Custom Flexible workflow with a class as leading object. Following the video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MQa4gMWlIic

I can determine the agents manually or with approver parameter. But I need to determine the agent via "Manage Teams and Responsibilities"  wtih this Custom Flexible workflow. ( Not standard PO or SO flexible workflows that are already ready to customize ).

How can I achieve that ?

Philippe

entry.content: Hey LEE325, did you find the resolution for this issue? I have same one and stuck in there.

entry.title: How can you work with in-process inspections in SAP Digital Manufacturing?
entry.content:
During production execution we want to do "sampling" quality inspections. These inspections are planned, For example, on thursday we want to 100% check all products on "assembly stop 3" for all data collection values.

I've read a lot about quality approval, inspection pass, special routings, data collections, assembly data, set points, visual inspections. but i cannot get a good sense how to configure this process. 

This looks pretty okay:

If i create a trigger for the completion of an SFC on an operation activity and start a special routing to start the quality process. How can I configure a check list with all the things to be checked by the quality employee? 

BTW: there is NO integration with ERP!!!

My main questions:

1. Trigger a quality employee to perform inspection on resource/operation/employee.

2. Provide quality employee with checklist

3. Make sure a product passes or fails an inspection.

entry.title: Access Control Concern Regarding Journal Entry Apps
entry.content:
Hello,

I have a question regarding the following apps: Verify General Journal Entries, Manage Journal Entries, and Post General Journal Entries.

For one of our customers, the intended process is that users should handle journal entries exclusively through the Verify General Journal Entries app. This approach is in place to ensure proper control and that approval workflows are followed before any postings are made.

However, we have identified that it is still possible to bypass this process.

Issue Identified:

  • The Manage Journal Entries app remains accessible through certain roles/catalogs
  • This app includes the "Create" function, which allows users to post journal entries directly—outside of the intended approval process.

Furthermore, the Post General Journal Entries app is also accessible via these roles/catalogs, enabling posting without prior verification.

This setup does not align with the defined access strategy for the customer and poses a risk of journal entries being posted without appropriate validation.

 

We have noted that the catalog SAP_FIN_BC_GL_CLOSING_PC cannot be removed, as it includes access to several apps that are actively used. However, the issue is that this catalog also grants access to the Manage Journal Entries app.

We would appreciate any guidance or recommendations on how to handle this situation while preserving access to the necessary apps.

entry.title: Re: Issue on Label Printing: the character 'O' (letter o) looks like a '0' (zero)
entry.content: @Sandra_Rossi I'm not really from technical so I was wondering how fonts are called within ABAP and how it is applied to the label command itself as an IPL language

entry.title: Re: Not possible to set the PO item returns indicator ret...
entry.content:
Hello Dipak,

enclosed you will find a short summary of the fields.

 

  1. low-cost

Meaning: The Low-cost field indicates that the costs of the respective purchase requisition process are classified as low. This often refers to the value of the purchase requisition or the amount of work required to process it.

Usage: You would use this field to identify requisitions that are low cost or low effort. It could help to process less critical requisitions faster, making the whole process more efficient.

 

  1. mdm-cost (master data management cost)

Meaning: The Mdm-cost field shows the costs incurred by master data management. These are the costs incurred for the maintenance and management of master data (such as supplier and article master data) used in the purchase requisition process.

Usage: This field is used to analyze how much costs are incurred under management of master data. These can be caused by the creation, maintenance, updating and cleansing of data. If these costs are high, this could indicate inefficient master data management processes that could be optimized.

 

  1. high-cost

Meaning: The High-cost field indicates that the costs of the purchase requisition process are classified as high. This can be caused by high values of the purchase requisition or by more complex processes that require more time and effort.

Usage: This field is useful for identifying requisitions that require special attention due to their high cost or effort. These may be expensive items or purchase requisitions with more complicated approval processes. A detailed analysis of these requisitions can help improve efficiency or identify expensive processes that should be optimized.

 

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: Re: Cleared in the meantime"error message
entry.content:
Hi Pyae,

I don't know if you still have this issue, but something that help me with this balance error was passing two documents for clearing, an invoice(RE) and a vendor payment(KZ). Check if you are passing two documents, and the sum of both documents should balance 0.00.

Kind Regards,

Marcelo R.

entry.title: How to sort the data on the BTP side in MDK app?
entry.content:
Fetching the table from ERP side with odata services with get entity set. There is an option where this data from the table can be modified, and once the data is modified, I want to display all the modified data on the top of the list. 

Is there any way to achieve this ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.content:

Kind regards

Michael

entry.title: Error Handling in SAP BPA Processes | How Should I Handle Build Process Errors?
entry.content:
Hello Builders,

During my recent builds in SAP Process Automation I have noticed there are considerably fewer resources for error handling in "processes" than in "automations." On the automations side there is a sub flow tab that allows you to pass on error details with additional flow steps. For example, you can add a notification form for your user containing the current error, allowing the automation to fail gracefully.

I currently have a process that contains an API Action that posts to an external service. When this Action fails it ends the process completely and does not pass outputs to any subsequent process steps. I can see the error in the monitoring tab but the end user, obviously, will not. How do you follow design principles in SAP BPA processes?

I searched the community and product documentation on this subject but could only find answers related to the "automation" side.

Any recommendations, guidance, or links to resources would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance,

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: LFM1 change fields definition
entry.content: Already discussed and solution given here

entry.title: Re: Fixed asset valuation - 0L and 2L ledgers both need t...
entry.content:
Hola Gabor5

I invite you to read the Asset Accounting documentation on the SAP Help Portal. I will share the links that answer your questions.

My questions are:

  • Do we need to fill in both ledgers? What happens if we don't?

Every time you run the depreciation at the end of the month, you select the ledger and the system automatically calculates those values based on the rules of each depreciation area. The system will create the journal entries for the corresponding depreciation areas.

  • What is the difference between these ledgers in this context?

When your SAP System was configured, the company decided to use 2 ledgers - one for Local purposes (ledger 2L) and the other to use the IFRS rules (Ledger 0L). 

However, each ledger has different depreciation rules predefined by country to meet various requirements, so you see more than one.

Find more details of the Manage Fixed Asset in this SAP Help Portal Link

  • Is there a best practice or recommendation for which valuation areas should be maintained, and which ones can be left alone if unused?

As I mentioned, the depreciation amount for this valuation areas are calculated automatically. You cannot deactive it and you cannot change predefined depreciation keys.

  • Why does Ledger 2L appear multiple times with different depreciation areas (15, 16, 32)?

Each depreciation area has different depreciation rules and it is use for different purposes, you can see the names:

  • 15 - Local Tax
  • 16 - Customer specific use
  • 32 - Book depreciation

I highly recommend you discuss this topic with your the Company's Accounting Department.

 

Best regards.

Edna

entry.title: BAS service missing in BTP subaccount entitlements
entry.content: In the global account, I created a sub-account with Сloud Foundry, but when trying to add BAS to the account's entitlements, there is no option to select Busines Application Studio (screenshot below). What could be the reason?

entry.title: Re: SFTP using Rise with SAP( no PI )
entry.content: Another option is to use the AWS SDK for SAP ABAP which lets you send and receive files via SFTP using Amazon S3 and AWS Transfer Family.  This solution is pure ABAP and requires no OS-level configuration.  Here's a blog which describes inbound and outbound scenarios: https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/awsforsap/secure-file-transfer-with-the-aws-sdk-for-sap-abap/

entry.title: LRN-191759 For this notification, is it triggered by the manager or just an overdue notification?
entry.content: LRN-191759 says "When this option is selected, overdue training reminders will not be sent to the recipient using the manager's email, but will use the Application Notification User Email instead." However, it does not indicate what is triggering the email. If this is a standard overdue notification, I would want the From email to be the application notification user email. If this is a notification triggered by the supervisor, I would want the from email to be the supervisor's. The documentation isn't clear.

entry.title: Re: When will prices for SQL Anywhere on the SAP Online Store be fixed?
entry.content: Thanks for helping, Chris. By "client only", do you mean the "remote database client"? As far as we're concerned, it could be either that or either of the two Edge edition offerings. It depends what we need for a particular customer. My concern now is that, if we have a customer needing SQL Anywhere licensing, we can't send them to the SQL Anywhere store because the pricing does not look right. We could have a need to do this at any time, and we don't want to have to tell our customers that we can't go ahead yet.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025
entry.content: It would be nice if it is decided that a 2025 C_BOBIP_ certificate will be offered, that existing C_BOBIP_43 certificate holders be given the opportunity to recertify instead of going through the entire process again.
